commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=f28b255948b255a3eb047650f0d677f8891b316c)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a dedicated direct-media HTTP client with timeouts and idle connection limits, routes `downloadDirectBytes` through it, and adds regression tests for client configuration and stalled-server behavior.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main reaches `downloadDirectBytes` from the read-only media command and uses `http.DefaultClient.Do(req)`, so a pre-header stall is only bounded by the caller context.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Needs maintainer review of the timeout policy and contributor-supplied real behavior proof before merge; the remaining proof gate cannot be completed by automation.

target.title: fix: bound direct media HTTP downloads
target.body:
## Summary

Bounds direct WhatsApp media downloads with an explicit HTTP client instead of `http.DefaultClient`.

The direct-media fallback already limits response body size and validates hashes/HMACs, but the transport path had no explicit total timeout, response-header timeout, idle timeout, or connection-pool bounds. A stalled media endpoint could hold a direct media download until the parent context is canceled.

## What changed

- Add a package-scoped direct media HTTP client.
- Set a 30s total request timeout.
- Set a 10s response-header timeout.
- Set idle connection timeout and bounded idle connection pool settings.
- Route `downloadDirectBytes` through the bounded client instead of `http.DefaultClient`.
- Add regression tests for configuration, client routing, and stalled-server behavior.

## Security impact

This reduces denial-of-service risk in long-running sync/media workflows. A media server or network path that accepts a request but never returns headers should no longer pin the direct media download path indefinitely.

## Behavior proof

### Pre-fix behavior

Before this change, `downloadDirectBytes` used the process-wide default client:

```go
resp, err := http.DefaultClient.Do(req)
```

`http.DefaultClient` has no default request timeout. The existing body limit only applies after response headers arrive and a body can be read. It does not bound a server that accepts the request and stalls before headers.

### Post-fix behavior

After this change, `downloadDirectBytes` uses the dedicated direct-media client:

```go
resp, err := directMediaHTTPClient.Do(req)
```

The production client sets a total request timeout, a response-header timeout, idle timeout, and bounded idle connection pool settings.

### Regression proof added in tests

`TestDownloadDirectBytesUsesDedicatedHTTPClient` replaces `directMediaHTTPClient` with a custom RoundTripper and calls `downloadDirectBytes`. The test asserts:

- the custom client was called
- the expected media URL was requested
- WhatsApp media headers are still attached
- the response body is read successfully

This proves `downloadDirectBytes` is actually routed through the dedicated client. If the implementation regresses back to `http.DefaultClient.Do(req)`, this test fails because the injected transport is never called.

`TestDownloadDirectBytesFailsFastWhenServerStalls` starts an `httptest.Server` that accepts the request and intentionally does not send a response. The test injects a 50ms client timeout and asserts `downloadDirectBytes` returns an error in under one second. This proves the direct media path fails within a bounded time when the server stalls.

`TestNewDirectMediaHTTPClientHasTimeouts` verifies the production client config has:

- positive total request timeout
- positive response-header timeout
- positive idle connection timeout
- bounded idle connection pool settings

## Tests

Not run in this ChatGPT environment because the container cannot resolve github.com to clone dependencies. Intended local checks:

```bash
go test ./internal/wa
go test ./...
```

Full maintainer check set:

```bash
test -z "$(gofmt -l internal/wa/media.go internal/wa/media_timeout_test.go)"
go test ./internal/wa
go test ./...
git diff --check
```
target.author: jason-allen-oneal
